title: Intentional latch warning -- regression?
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

verilator v5.030 reports an warning on the following latch code which was not present in earlier versions.  I believe best practice for intentional latches is a non-blocking statement, so the warning is erroneous (correct me if I'm wrong). http://www.sunburst-design.com/papers/CummingsSNUG2000SJ_NBA_rev1_2.pdf

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contaned so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

```
module bsg_dlatch #(parameter width_p = 0
                   )
  (input               clk_i
  ,input [width_p-1:0] data_i
  ,output logic [width_p-1:0] data_o
  );

  logic [width_p-1:0] data_r;
  always_latch
      if (clk_i)
        data_r <= data_i;

  assign data_o = data_r;

endmodule
```


```
%Warning-COMBDLY: /mnt/users/ssd0/homes/petrisko/research/black-parrot-sim/black-parrot/external/       basejump_stl/bsg_misc/bsg_dlatch.sv:18:16: Non-blocking assignment '<=' in combinational logic process

                                  : ... This will be executed as a blocking assignment '='!
   18 |         data_r <= data_i;
      |                ^~
                  ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/COMBDLY?v=5.030
                  ... Use "/* verilator lint_off COMBDLY */" and lint_on around source to disable this  message.
                  *** See https://verilator.org/warn/COMBDLY before disabling this,
                  else you may end up with different sim results.
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```verilator --cc --lint-only bsg_dlatch.sv```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

Verilator 4.221 devel rev v4.220-4-g14d24213 -- no warning

Verilator 5.030 2024-10-27 rev v5.030 -- has warning

What OS and distribution are you using?

```
[petrisko@kk9 syn]$ lsb_release --all
LSB Version:	:core-4.1-amd64:core-4.1-ia32:core-4.1-noarch:cxx-4.1-amd64:cxx-4.1-ia32:cxx-4.1-noarch:desktop-4.1-amd64:desktop-4.1-ia32:desktop-4.1-noarch:languages-4.1-amd64:languages-4.1-noarch:printing-4.1-amd64:printing-4.1-noarch
Distributor ID:	CentOS
Description:	CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)
Release:	7.9.2009
Codename:	Core
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Happy to help

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
